__comment_spec: Spec 061 SCOPE-09 dashboard fragment per design.md §8.4. Seven panels render the closed-vocabulary metrics declared in internal/assistant/metrics/metrics.go. Cardinality is bounded on every series so the dashboard remains usable across all transports and scenarios. Importable into an existing Grafana instance via the Grafana HTTP API (POST /api/dashboards/db) or via Grafana's filesystem provider. Not wired into deploy/compose.deploy.yml in this repo because Grafana is not a project-owned runtime; the deploy adapter overlay binds it.
title: Smackerel — Assistant Capability (spec 061)

panels.title: Per-transport turn volume + band mix
panels.description: spec 061 design §8.4 panel 1. Rate of facade turns broken down by transport (Y axis) and router band (color). Sudden borderline spikes hint at a manifest gap; sudden low spikes hint at a routing-floor misconfiguration.

panels.title: Per-scenario success vs failure
panels.description: spec 061 design §8.4 panel 2. Rate of skill invocations split by outcome. The OK series rising while {timeout, provider_error, schema_failure} stay flat is the green-line shape.

panels.title: Capture-as-fallback rate (by cause)
panels.description: spec 061 design §8.4 panel 3. Rate of capture-fallback events split by cause. A sudden borderline_timeout or confirm_timeout climb means the operator-visible UX is degrading without throwing an error.

panels.title: Provenance violations (alert at >0)
panels.description: spec 061 design §8.4 panel 4. Provenance-gate violation counter. SLO target = 0 over any window; any non-zero value is a routable alert (gate fired = a scenario tried to answer without a Source).

panels.title: Active conversation threads per transport
panels.description: spec 061 design §8.4 panel 5. Gauge of active assistant_conversations rows per transport. The gauge is refreshed by the context store's idle-sweep ticker; a sustained climb past historical baseline indicates the idle TTL is too long (or context isn't being cleared on /reset).

panels.title: Confirm-card outcome distribution
panels.description: spec 061 design §8.4 panel 6. Rate of confirm-card terminal outcomes. discarded_timeout climbing is the natural alert: it means users are seeing a card but the offer-window is too short OR the UX is unclear.

panels.title: Source-assembly drops (graph drift)
panels.description: spec 061 design §8.4 panel 7. Rate of cited-artifact drops by cause. missing_artifact climbing means graph drift between retrieval and synthesis (BS-007 path); lookup_error climbing means a transient store outage. Both should reconcile with provenance-violation events when ALL citations drop.
